label: Tool
name: Agent
description: Spawns a subagent with its own context window to handle a task

label: Tool
name: AskUserQuestion
description: Asks multiple-choice questions to gather requirements or clarify ambiguity

label: Tool
name: Bash
description: Executes shell commands in your environment. See Bash tool behavior

label: Tool
name: CronCreate
description: Schedules a recurring or one-shot prompt within the current session. Tasks are session-scoped and restored on `--resume` or `--continue` if unexpired. See scheduled tasks

label: Tool
name: CronDelete
description: Cancels a scheduled task by ID

label: Tool
name: CronList
description: Lists all scheduled tasks in the session

label: Tool
name: Edit
description: Makes targeted edits to specific files

label: Tool
name: EnterPlanMode
description: Switches to plan mode to design an approach before coding

label: Tool
name: EnterWorktree
description: Creates an isolated git worktree and switches into it. Pass a `path` to switch into an existing worktree of the current repository instead of creating a new one. Not available to subagents

label: Tool
name: ExitPlanMode
description: Presents a plan for approval and exits plan mode

label: Tool
name: ExitWorktree
description: Exits a worktree session and returns to the original directory. Not available to subagents

label: Tool
name: Glob
description: Finds files based on pattern matching

label: Tool
name: Grep
description: Searches for patterns in file contents

label: Tool
name: ListMcpResourcesTool
description: Lists resources exposed by connected MCP servers

label: Tool
name: LSP
description: Code intelligence via language servers: jump to definitions, find references, report type errors and warnings. See LSP tool behavior

label: Tool
name: Monitor
description: Runs a command in the background and feeds each output line back to Claude, so it can react to log entries, file changes, or polled status mid-conversation. See Monitor tool

label: Tool
name: NotebookEdit
description: Modifies Jupyter notebook cells

label: Tool
name: PowerShell
description: Executes PowerShell commands natively. See PowerShell tool for availability

label: Tool
name: Read
description: Reads the contents of files

label: Tool
name: ReadMcpResourceTool
description: Reads a specific MCP resource by URI

label: Tool
name: SendMessage
description: Sends a message to an agent team teammate, or resumes a subagent by its agent ID. Stopped subagents auto-resume in the background. Only available when `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` is set

label: Tool
name: Skill
description: Executes a skill within the main conversation

label: Tool
name: TaskCreate
description: Creates a new task in the task list

label: Tool
name: TaskGet
description: Retrieves full details for a specific task

label: Tool
name: TaskList
description: Lists all tasks with their current status

label: Tool
name: TaskOutput
description: (Deprecated) Retrieves output from a background task. Prefer `Read` on the task's output file path

label: Tool
name: TaskStop
description: Kills a running background task by ID

label: Tool
name: TaskUpdate
description: Updates task status, dependencies, details, or deletes tasks

label: Tool
name: TeamCreate
description: Creates an agent team with multiple teammates. Only available when `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` is set

label: Tool
name: TeamDelete
description: Disbands an agent team and cleans up teammate processes. Only available when `CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS=1` is set

label: Tool
name: TodoWrite
description: Manages the session task checklist. Available in non-interactive mode and the Agent SDK; interactive sessions use TaskCreate, TaskGet, TaskList, and TaskUpdate instead

label: Tool
name: ToolSearch
description: Searches for and loads deferred tools when tool search is enabled

label: Tool
name: WebFetch
description: Fetches content from a specified URL

label: Tool
name: WebSearch
description: Performs web searches

label: Tool
name: Write
description: Creates or overwrites files

label: SettingKey
description: Run the main thread as a named subagent. Applies that subagent's system prompt, tool restrictions, and model. See Invoke subagents explicitly

label: SettingKey
description: (Managed settings only) Allowlist of channel plugins that may push messages. Replaces the default Anthropic allowlist when set. Undefined = fall back to the default, empty array = block all channel plugins. Requires `channelsEnabled: true`. See Restrict which channel plugins can run

label: SettingKey
description: Allowlist of URL patterns that HTTP hooks may target. Supports `*` as a wildcard. When set, hooks with non-matching URLs are blocked. Undefined = no restriction, empty array = block all HTTP hooks. Arrays merge across settings sources. See Hook configuration

label: SettingKey
description: When set in managed-settings.json, allowlist of MCP servers users can configure. Undefined = no restrictions, empty array = lockdown. Applies to all scopes. Denylist takes precedence. See Managed MCP configuration

label: SettingKey
description: (Managed settings only) Only managed hooks, SDK hooks, and hooks from plugins force-enabled in managed settings `enabledPlugins` are loaded. User, project, and all other plugin hooks are blocked. See Hook configuration

label: SettingKey
description: (Managed settings only) Only `allowedMcpServers` from managed settings are respected. `deniedMcpServers` still merges from all sources. Users can still add MCP servers, but only the admin-defined allowlist applies. See Managed MCP configuration

label: SettingKey
description: (Managed settings only) Prevent user and project settings from defining `allow`, `ask`, or `deny` permission rules. Only rules in managed settings apply. See Managed-only settings

label: SettingKey
description: Enable extended thinking by default for all sessions. Typically configured via the `/config` command rather than editing directly

label: SettingKey
description: Custom script, to be executed in `/bin/sh`, to generate an auth value. This value will be sent as `X-Api-Key` and `Authorization: Bearer` headers for model requests

label: SettingKey
description: Customize attribution for git commits and pull requests. See Attribution settings

label: SettingKey
description: Custom directory for auto memory storage. Accepts `~/`-expanded paths. Not accepted in project settings (`.claude/settings.json`) to prevent shared repos from redirecting memory writes to sensitive locations. Accepted from policy, local, and user settings

label: SettingKey
description: Customize what the auto mode classifier blocks and allows. Contains `environment`, `allow`, and `soft_deny` arrays of prose rules. Include the literal string `"$defaults"` in an array to inherit the built-in rules at that position. See Configure auto mode. Not read from shared project settings

label: SettingKey
description: In fullscreen rendering, follow new output to the bottom of the conversation. Default: `true`. Appears in `/config` as Auto-scroll. Permission prompts still scroll into view when this is off

label: SettingKey
description: Release channel to follow for updates. Use `"stable"` for a version that is typically about one week old and skips versions with major regressions, or `"latest"` (default) for the most recent release

label: SettingKey
description: Restrict which models users can select via `/model`, `--model`, or `ANTHROPIC_MODEL`. Does not affect the Default option. See Restrict model selection

label: SettingKey
description: Show a one-line session recap when you return to the terminal after a few minutes away. Set to `false` or turn off Session recap in `/config` to disable. Same as `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AWAY_SUMMARY`

label: SettingKey
description: Custom script that modifies the `.aws` directory (see advanced credential configuration)

label: SettingKey
description: Custom script that outputs JSON with AWS credentials (see advanced credential configuration)

label: SettingKey
description: (Managed settings only) Blocklist of marketplace sources. Enforced on marketplace add and on plugin install, update, refresh, and auto-update, so a marketplace added before the policy was set cannot be used to fetch plugins. Blocked sources are checked before downloading, so they never touch the filesystem. See Managed marketplace restrictions

label: SettingKey
description: (Managed settings only) Allow channels for Team and Enterprise users. Unset or `false` blocks channel message delivery regardless of what users pass to `--channels`

label: SettingKey
description: Session files older than this period are deleted at startup (default: 30 days, minimum 1). Setting to `0` is rejected with a validation error. Also controls the age cutoff for automatic removal of orphaned subagent worktrees at startup. To disable transcript writes entirely, set the `CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_PROMPT_HISTORY` environment variable, or in non-interactive mode (`-p`) use the `--no-session-persistence` flag or the `persistSession: false` SDK option.

label: SettingKey
description: Announcement to display to users at startup. If multiple announcements are provided, they will be cycled through at random.

label: SettingKey
description: Default shell for input-box `!` commands. Accepts `"bash"` (default) or `"powershell"`. Setting `"powershell"` routes interactive `!` commands through PowerShell on Windows. Requires `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_POWERSHELL_TOOL=1`. See PowerShell tool

label: SettingKey
description: When set in managed-settings.json, denylist of MCP servers that are explicitly blocked. Applies to all scopes including managed servers. Denylist takes precedence over allowlist. See Managed MCP configuration

label: SettingKey
description: Disable all hooks and any custom status line

label: SettingKey
description: Set to `"disable"` to prevent auto mode from being activated. Removes `auto` from the `Shift+Tab` cycle and rejects `--permission-mode auto` at startup. Most useful in managed settings where users cannot override it

label: SettingKey
description: Set to `"disable"` to prevent Claude Code from registering the `claude-cli://` protocol handler with the operating system on startup. Deep links let external tools open a Claude Code session with a pre-filled prompt via `claude-cli://open?q=...`. The `q` parameter supports multi-line prompts using URL-encoded newlines (`%0A`). Useful in environments where protocol handler registration is restricted or managed separately

label: SettingKey
description: List of specific MCP servers from `.mcp.json` files to reject

label: SettingKey
description: Disable inline shell execution for `!`...`` and ````!` blocks in skills and custom commands from user, project, plugin, or additional-directory sources. Commands are replaced with `[shell command execution disabled by policy]` instead of being run. Bundled and managed skills are not affected. Most useful in managed settings where users cannot override it

label: SettingKey
description: Key binding mode for the input prompt: `"normal"` or `"vim"`. Default: `"normal"`. Appears in `/config` as Editor mode

label: SettingKey
description: Persist the effort level across sessions. Accepts `"low"`, `"medium"`, `"high"`, or `"xhigh"`. Written automatically when you run `/effort` with one of those values. See Adjust effort level for supported models

label: SettingKey
description: Automatically approve all MCP servers defined in project `.mcp.json` files

label: SettingKey
description: List of specific MCP servers from `.mcp.json` files to approve

label: SettingKey
description: Environment variables that will be applied to every session

label: SettingKey
description: When `true`, fast mode does not persist across sessions. Each session starts with fast mode off, requiring users to enable it with `/fast`. The user's fast mode preference is still saved. See Require per-session opt-in

label: SettingKey
description: Probability (0–1) that the session quality survey appears when eligible. Set to `0` to suppress entirely. Useful when using Bedrock, Vertex, or Foundry where the default sample rate does not apply

label: SettingKey
description: Configure a custom script for `@` file autocomplete. See File suggestion settings

label: SettingKey
description: Use `claudeai` to restrict login to Claude.ai accounts, `console` to restrict login to Claude Console (API usage billing) accounts

label: SettingKey
description: Require login to belong to a specific organization. Accepts a single UUID string, which also pre-selects that organization during login, or an array of UUIDs where any listed organization is accepted without pre-selection. When set in managed settings, login fails if the authenticated account does not belong to a listed organization; an empty array fails closed and blocks login with a misconfiguration message

label: SettingKey
description: (Managed settings only) Block CLI startup until remote managed settings are freshly fetched from the server. If the fetch fails, the CLI exits rather than continuing with cached or no settings. When not set, startup continues without waiting for remote settings. See fail-closed enforcement

label: SettingKey
description: Configure custom commands to run at lifecycle events. See hooks documentation for format

label: SettingKey
description: Allowlist of environment variable names HTTP hooks may interpolate into headers. When set, each hook's effective `allowedEnvVars` is the intersection with this list. Undefined = no restriction. Arrays merge across settings sources. See Hook configuration

label: SettingKey
description: Deprecated: Use `attribution` instead. Whether to include the `co-authored-by Claude` byline in git commits and pull requests (default: `true`)

label: SettingKey
description: Include built-in commit and PR workflow instructions and the git status snapshot in Claude's system prompt (default: `true`). Set to `false` to remove both, for example when using your own git workflow skills. The `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_GIT_INSTRUCTIONS` environment variable takes precedence over this setting when set

label: SettingKey
description: Configure Claude's preferred response language (e.g., `"japanese"`, `"spanish"`, `"french"`). Claude will respond in this language by default. Also sets the voice dictation language

label: SettingKey
description: Floor that prevents background auto-updates and `claude update` from installing a version below this one. Switching from the `"latest"` channel to `"stable"` via `/config` prompts you to stay on the current version or allow the downgrade. Choosing to stay sets this value. Also useful in managed settings to pin an organization-wide minimum

label: SettingKey
description: Override the default model to use for Claude Code

label: SettingKey
description: Map Anthropic model IDs to provider-specific model IDs such as Bedrock inference profile ARNs. Each model picker entry uses its mapped value when calling the provider API. See Override model IDs per version

label: SettingKey
description: Script to generate dynamic OpenTelemetry headers. Runs at startup and periodically (see Dynamic headers)

label: SettingKey
description: Configure an output style to adjust the system prompt. See output styles documentation

label: SettingKey
description: See table below for structure of permissions.

label: SettingKey
description: Customize where plan files are stored. Path is relative to project root. Default: `~/.claude/plans`

label: SettingKey
description: (Managed settings only) Custom message appended to the plugin trust warning shown before installation. Use this to add organization-specific context, for example to confirm that plugins from your internal marketplace are vetted.

label: SettingKey
description: Reduce or disable UI animations (spinners, shimmer, flash effects) for accessibility

label: SettingKey
description: URL template for the PR badge shown in the footer and in tool-result summaries. Substitutes `{host}`, `{owner}`, `{repo}`, `{number}`, and `{url}` from the `gh`-reported PR URL. Use to point PR links at an internal code-review tool instead of `github.com`. Does not affect `#123` autolinks in Claude's prose

label: SettingKey
description: Control whether the `@` file picker respects `.gitignore` patterns. When `true` (default), files matching `.gitignore` patterns are excluded from suggestions

label: SettingKey
description: Show the "clear context" option on the plan accept screen. Defaults to `false`. Set to `true` to restore the option

label: SettingKey
description: Show extended thinking summaries in interactive sessions. When unset or `false` (default in interactive mode), thinking blocks are redacted by the API and shown as a collapsed stub. Redaction only changes what you see, not what the model generates: to reduce thinking spend, lower the budget or disable thinking instead. Non-interactive mode (`-p`) and SDK callers always receive summaries regardless of this setting

label: SettingKey
description: Show turn duration messages after responses, e.g. "Cooked for 1m 6s". Default: `true`. Appears in `/config` as Show turn duration

label: SettingKey
description: Skip the WebFetch domain safety check that sends each requested hostname to `api.anthropic.com` before fetching. Set to `true` in environments that block traffic to Anthropic, such as Bedrock, Vertex AI, or Foundry deployments with restrictive egress. When skipped, WebFetch attempts any URL without consulting the blocklist

label: SettingKey
description: Show tips in the spinner while Claude is working. Set to `false` to disable tips (default: `true`)

label: SettingKey
description: Override spinner tips with custom strings. `tips`: array of tip strings. `excludeDefault`: if `true`, only show custom tips; if `false` or absent, custom tips are merged with built-in tips

label: SettingKey
description: Customize the action verbs shown in the spinner and turn duration messages. Set `mode` to `"replace"` to use only your verbs, or `"append"` to add them to the defaults

label: SettingKey
description: SSH connections to show in the Desktop environment dropdown. Each entry requires `id`, `name`, and `sshHost`; `sshPort`, `sshIdentityFile`, and `startDirectory` are optional. When set in managed settings, connections are read-only for users. Read from managed and user settings only

label: SettingKey
description: Configure a custom status line to display context. See `statusLine` documentation

label: SettingKey
description: (Managed settings only) Allowlist of plugin marketplace sources. Undefined = no restrictions, empty array = lockdown. Enforced on marketplace add and on plugin install, update, refresh, and auto-update, so a marketplace added before the policy was set cannot be used to fetch plugins. See Managed marketplace restrictions

label: SettingKey
description: How agent team teammates display: `auto` (picks split panes in tmux or iTerm2, in-process otherwise), `in-process`, or `tmux`. See choose a display mode

label: SettingKey
description: Show the terminal progress bar in supported terminals: ConEmu, Ghostty 1.2.0+, and iTerm2 3.6.6+. Default: `true`. Appears in `/config` as Terminal progress bar

label: SettingKey
description: Terminal UI renderer. Use `"fullscreen"` for the flicker-free alt-screen renderer with virtualized scrollback. Use `"default"` for the classic main-screen renderer. Set via `/tui`

label: SettingKey
description: Whether plan mode uses auto mode semantics when auto mode is available. Default: `true`. Not read from shared project settings. Appears in `/config` as "Use auto mode during plan"

label: SettingKey
description: Default transcript view mode on startup: `"default"`, `"verbose"`, or `"focus"`. Overrides the sticky `/focus` selection when set

label: SettingKey
description: Voice dictation settings: `enabled` turns dictation on, `mode` selects `"hold"` or `"tap"`, and `autoSubmit` sends the prompt on key release in hold mode. Written automatically when you run `/voice`. Requires a Claude.ai account

label: SettingKey
description: Legacy alias for `voice.enabled`. Prefer the `voice` object

label: SettingKey
description: (Windows managed settings only) When `true`, Claude Code on WSL reads managed settings from the Windows policy chain in addition to `/etc/claude-code`, with Windows sources taking priority. Only honored when set in the HKLM registry key or `C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\managed-settings.json`, both of which require Windows admin to write. For HKCU policy to also apply on WSL, the flag must additionally be set in HKCU itself. Has no effect on native Windows

label: SettingKey
description: Automatically connect to a running IDE when Claude Code starts from an external terminal. Default: `false`. Appears in `/config` as Auto-connect to IDE (external terminal) when running outside a VS Code or JetBrains terminal

label: SettingKey
description: Automatically install the Claude Code IDE extension when running from a VS Code terminal. Default: `true`. Appears in `/config` as Auto-install IDE extension when running inside a VS Code or JetBrains terminal. You can also set the `CLAUDE_CODE_IDE_SKIP_AUTO_INSTALL` environment variable

label: SettingKey
description: Prepend Claude's previous response as `#`-commented context when you open the external editor with `Ctrl+G`. Default: `false`. Appears in `/config` as Show last response in external editor

label: SettingKey
description: Directories to symlink from the main repository into each worktree to avoid duplicating large directories on disk. No directories are symlinked by default

label: SettingKey
description: Directories to check out in each worktree via git sparse-checkout (cone mode). Only the listed paths are written to disk, which is faster in large monorepos

label: SettingKey
description: When `true`, only `filesystem.allowRead` paths from managed settings are respected. `denyRead` still merges from all sources

label: SettingKey
description: When `true`, only `allowedDomains` and `WebFetch(domain:...)` allow rules from managed settings are respected. Non-allowed domains are blocked automatically without prompting the user. Denied domains still merge from all sources

label: Hook
name: SessionStart
description: When a session begins or resumes

label: Hook
name: Setup
description: When you start Claude Code with `--init-only`, or with `--init` or `--maintenance` in `-p` mode. For one-time preparation in CI or scripts

label: Hook
name: UserPromptSubmit
description: When you submit a prompt, before Claude processes it

label: Hook
name: UserPromptExpansion
description: When a user-typed command expands into a prompt, before it reaches Claude. Can block the expansion

label: Hook
name: PreToolUse
description: Before a tool call executes. Can block it

label: Hook
name: PermissionRequest
description: When a permission dialog appears

label: Hook
name: PermissionDenied
description: When a tool call is denied by the auto mode classifier. Return `{retry: true}` to tell the model it may retry the denied tool call

label: Hook
name: PostToolUse
description: After a tool call succeeds

label: Hook
name: PostToolUseFailure
description: After a tool call fails

label: Hook
name: PostToolBatch
description: After a full batch of parallel tool calls resolves, before the next model call

label: Hook
name: Notification
description: When Claude Code sends a notification

label: Hook
name: SubagentStart
description: When a subagent is spawned

label: Hook
name: SubagentStop
description: When a subagent finishes

label: Hook
name: TaskCreated
description: When a task is being created via `TaskCreate`

label: Hook
name: TaskCompleted
description: When a task is being marked as completed

label: Hook
name: Stop
description: When Claude finishes responding

label: Hook
name: StopFailure
description: When the turn ends due to an API error. Output and exit code are ignored

label: Hook
name: TeammateIdle
description: When an agent team teammate is about to go idle

label: Hook
name: InstructionsLoaded
description: When a CLAUDE.md or `.claude/rules/*.md` file is loaded into context. Fires at session start and when files are lazily loaded during a session

label: Hook
name: ConfigChange
description: When a configuration file changes during a session

label: Hook
name: CwdChanged
description: When the working directory changes, for example when Claude executes a `cd` command. Useful for reactive environment management with tools like direnv

label: Hook
name: FileChanged
description: When a watched file changes on disk. The `matcher` field specifies which filenames to watch

label: Hook
name: WorktreeCreate
description: When a worktree is being created via `--worktree` or `isolation: "worktree"`. Replaces default git behavior

label: Hook
name: WorktreeRemove
description: When a worktree is being removed, either at session exit or when a subagent finishes

label: Hook
name: PreCompact
description: Before context compaction

label: Hook
name: PostCompact
description: After context compaction completes

label: Hook
name: Elicitation
description: When an MCP server requests user input during a tool call

label: Hook
name: ElicitationResult
description: After a user responds to an MCP elicitation, before the response is sent back to the server

label: Hook
name: SessionEnd
description: When a session terminates

label: PermissionMode
name: default
description: Reads only

label: PermissionMode
name: acceptEdits
description: Reads, file edits, and common filesystem commands (`mkdir`, `touch`, `mv`, `cp`, etc.)

label: PermissionMode
name: plan
description: Reads only

label: PermissionMode
name: auto
description: Everything, with background safety checks

label: PermissionMode
name: dontAsk
description: Only pre-approved tools

label: PermissionMode
name: bypassPermissions
description: Everything except protected paths
